allow_instructions: Read-only filesystem operations are fine: ls, cat, head, tail, wc, grep, rg, find, file size checks.
Type checks, lints, and test runs are fine: tsc, vitest, npm test, npm run test, npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run verify:build.
Package manager read and in-repo install commands are fine: npm ls, npm view, npm outdated, npm install, npm ci when run inside the HourDen repo.
Git read commands are fine: git status, git diff, git log, git show. Git fetch is fine.
Local Docker Compose for dev is fine: docker compose up, docker compose start, docker compose ps, docker compose logs against the repo docker-compose.yml.
MCP read calls against documentation, search, design inspection, and internal wikis are fine.
Browser MCP against localhost:5173 and localhost:3001 for local dev verification is fine.
Read-only production checks are fine: curl or fetch to hourden.hannesduve.com health or verify-production scripts that only inspect state.
Fetch calls to github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com, npmjs.com, registry.npmjs.org, cursor.com, nodejs.org, vite.dev, hono.dev, vitest.dev, postgresql.org, react.dev, and tailwindcss.com are fine.
block_instructions: Anything that writes to .env, .env.local, .env.example secrets, or other credential files requires approval.
npm run migrate, npm run create-user, and any psql or SQL against a non-localhost DATABASE_URL require approval.
Any git commit, git push, git reset --hard, git clean -fd, git rebase, or branch deletion requires approval.
Any ssh, scp, rsync, or scripts/deploy-remote, setup-caddy-vm, fix-caddy-vm, or fix-caddy-routes-vm requires approval.
Any docker compose or docker command targeting /opt/HourDen, /opt/Portfolio, or remote production hosts requires approval.
Any MCP call that deploys or mutates AWS infrastructure (plugin-deploy-on-aws-awsiac) requires approval.
Figma write tools (use_figma, create_new_file, generate_figma_design, upload_assets) require approval.
Browser MCP that logs into or submits forms on hourden.hannesduve.com requires approval.
Any installation command that touches global state (npm install -g, brew install, pipx install) requires approval.
Any fetch call to an unknown external host requires approval.
